title: Cauliflower & Broccoli Cheese
summary: The thing cauliflower cheese is missing is chorizo, which imparts a delicious, smokey flavour to the cheesy white sauce. This will become a household staple. In fact, cauliflower & broccoli cheese is undeniably a cult classic; if it makes you think of school dinners, think again. This recipe is jam-packed with a rich, cheesy sauce, plenty of chorizo, hefty amounts of broccoli, and just a hint of rosemary. It’s really, really tasty, so give it a go for dinner tonight and let us know what you think.

method.description: Crumble your chorizo into a pan and fry on a medium heat until crispy.
Add the sliced garlic cloves and fry for 30 more seconds. Remove the garlic and chorizo from the pan, leaving the oil.

Add a splash of olive oil to the pan along with plain flour. Beat it together with a whisk and once it has formed a roux (once it has combined) start adding your milk. Add it bit by bit, beating it in. Once you’ve added it all, you should have a nice thick white sauce.
Add almost all of your grated cheddar and grated Parmesan, reserving a little, some salt and a lot of pepper. Mix it in. Once melted, re-add your chorizo and garlic. Stir it in.
Break your cauliflower and broccoli into florets, add to the sauce and give it a good stir.
Once everything is evenly coated, top the dish with the remaining grated cheddar, Parmesan, a handful of rosemary leaves and a large handful of breadcrumbs.
Place in the oven for 50 minutes until nice and golden. Serve it up with a simple side salad and enjoy!

chefs.name: Ben Lebus
chefs.summary: Ben Lebus is the CEO and founder of Mob. Ben has always been passionate about food and learnt to cook from his father, who ran an Italian restaurant for a number of years. You can regularly find him cooking (and eating) some sort of puttanesca.
